text.title: django-selectors Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: locate query → extract → optimise → test → wire all callers.

step.name: locate-query
step.input: view / task / admin / serializer source file
step.action: Grep for `Model.objects` and `Model._default_manager`. List every call that filters, orders, or paginates the same model.
step.output: list of duplicate or near-duplicate query expressions
step.decision-gate: If the query appears in exactly one place AND has no related-object access, skip extraction.

step.name: design-signature
step.action: Pick the union of filter params used at the call sites. Signature: `def <entity>_<shape>(*, param1, param2=None) -> QuerySet[Model]`. Shape is `list`, `get`, `count_for`, `summary_for`, etc.

step.name: optimise
step.action: Add select_related for every FK/OneToOne the callers dereference; prefetch_related for every M2M / reverse FK. Use Prefetch(queryset=...) when the related queryset needs filtering or its own select_related.
step.decision-gate: Run nplusone middleware in tests — must report zero false N+1.

step.name: test
step.action: Add `tests/test_selectors.py` with `django_assert_num_queries(N)` locking the count, plus a basic correctness assertion (filter behaviour, ordering).
step.decision-gate: Deliberately remove a prefetch — test must fail; restore it — test must pass.

step.name: wire-callers
step.action: Replace each in-line query with a call to the selector. Drop any redundant select_related/prefetch_related from the caller side.
step.output: views/tasks/admin become thin; query logic centralised
step.decision-gate: All callers green; original tests still pass.
